articles.author: Brendan Byrne
articles.title: Artemis II astronauts swung by the moon, broke an Apollo record, and saw an eclipse - npr.org
articles.description: The NASA moon mission completed several key milestones as its crew looped around the lunar body. It's expected to splash down on Earth on Friday.
articles.content:
The four-astronaut crew of NASA's Artemis II mission is now on its way back to Earth after a record-breaking trip around the moon, traveling farther than any other humans have before.

articles.title: Artemis II crew tear up over idea of naming moon crater after astronaut’s late wife - thebanner.com
articles.description: Artemis II crew members want to name a moon crater after fellow astronaut Reid Wiseman’s late wife, Carroll.
articles.content:
While breaking the record for distance traveled by humans from Earth, the Artemis II crew members remain focused on more personal matters in the names theyve recommended for craters.

articles.author: Jorge Garay
articles.title: 5 Mysteries That the Artemis Missions to the Moon Could Finally Solve - WIRED
articles.description: The moon is not just a barren rock orbiting the Earth. The Artemis missions could answer the great unknowns that the satellite holds.
articles.content: For half a century humans thought they understood the moon: a static, airless, waterless landscape without many mysteries to solve. But orbiting instruments and robotic missions have proven otherwise… [+5159 chars]
